message.content: @web/src/pages/privacy-policy.tsx の内容を @policy.md の内容になるように修正して

    13→        content: '株式会社Knowhere（以下「当社」といいます）は、当社が提供するサービス「SmartScout」（以下「本サービス」といいます）においてユーザーの個人情報を適切に取り扱うために、本プライバシーポリシー（以下「本ポリシー」といいます）を定めます。なお、当社は個人情報の保護に関する法律その他の関連法令を遵守し、ユーザーの個人情報を適切に管理・保護いたします。\n\n本ポリシーにおいて「個人情報保護法」とは、個人情報の保護に関する法律を意味し、「個人情報」とは、個人情報保護法第2条第1項により定義される個人情報を意味し、「本人」とは、個人情報の主体である個人を意味するものとします。'

   133→        title: 'Article 14 (Effective Date and Revision Date)',
   134→        content: 'Effective Date: 2025/4/18\n\nThis Privacy Policy is originally written in Japanese. This English translation is provided for reference purposes. In the event of any inconsistency or discrepancy between the Japanese version and this English version, the Japanese version shall prevail.'
   135→      }
   136→    ]
   137→  }
   138→};
   139→
   140→function PageInner() {
   141→  const router = useRouter();
   142→  const [currentLang, setCurrentLang] = useState<'ja' | 'en'>('en');
   143→
   144→  useEffect(() => {
   145→    // クエリパラメータから言語を取得
   146→    const lang = router.query.lang as string;
   147→    if (lang === 'ja' || lang === 'en') {
   148→      setCurrentLang(lang);
   149→    }
   150→  }, [router.query.lang]);
   151→
   152→  const handleLanguageChange = (lang: 'ja' | 'en') => {
   153→    setCurrentLang(lang);
   154→    // URLのクエリパラメータも更新
   155→    router.push(`/privacy-policy?lang=${lang}`, undefined, { shallow: true });
   156→  };
   157→
   158→  const content = privacyPolicyContent[currentLang];
   159→
   160→  return (
   161→    <Box
   162→      sx={{
   163→        px: { xs: 2, sm: 4, md: 6 },
   164→        py: 2,
   165→      }}
   166→    >
   167→      <Box sx={{
   168→        display: 'flex',
   169→        flexDirection: { xs: 'column', sm: 'row' },
   170→        justifyContent: 'space-between',
   171→        alignItems: { xs: 'flex-start', sm: 'center' },
   172→        gap: { xs: 2, sm: 0 },
   173→        mb: 3
   174→      }}>
   175→        <Typography variant="h2" sx={{
   176→          fontSize: { xs: '1.75rem', sm: '2rem', md: '2.25rem' }
   177→        }}>
   178→          {content.title}
   179→        </Typography>
   180→        <Stack direction="row" spacing={1}>
   181→          <Button
   182→            variant={currentLang === 'en' ? 'contained' : 'outlined'}
   183→            onClick={() => handleLanguageChange('en')}
   184→            size="small"
   185→          >
   186→            English
   187→          </Button>
   188→          <Button
   189→            variant={currentLang === 'ja' ? 'contained' : 'outlined'}
   190→            onClick={() => handleLanguageChange('ja')}
   191→            size="small"
   192→          >
   193→            日本語
   194→          </Button>
   195→        </Stack>
   196→      </Box>
   197→      <Stack direction="column" justifyContent="center" spacing={3}>
   198→        {content.sections.map((section, index) => (
   199→          <Box key={index}>
   200→            <Typography variant="h6" sx={{
   201→              mb: 1,
   202→              fontWeight: 'bold',
   203→              fontSize: { xs: '1.1rem', sm: '1.25rem' }
   204→            }}>
   205→              {section.title}
   206→            </Typography>
   207→            <Typography sx={{
   208→              whiteSpace: 'pre-wrap',
   209→              fontSize: { xs: '0.875rem', sm: '1rem' },
   210→              lineHeight: { xs: 1.5, sm: 1.6 }
   211→            }}>
   212→              {section.content}
   213→            </Typography>
   214→          </Box>
   215→        ))}
   216→      </Stack>
   217→    </Box>
   218→  );
   219→}
   220→
   221→export default function Page() {
   222→  return <PageInner />;
   223→}
   224→
   225→Page.getLayout = function getLayout(page: ReactElement) {
   226→  return <DocumentsLayout tab="privacy">{page}</DocumentsLayout>;
   227→};

1→SmartScout Privacy Policy

(hereinafter the "Company") collects, uses, and manages Personal Information of users (hereinafter "User(s)") in relation to the "SmartScout" service (hereinafter the "Service").     39→The Service may use cookies, web beacons, and similar tracking technologies to enhance User experience and analyze User behavior. Cookies are small data files stored on a User’s device that allow the Service to remember the User’s preferences and activities. Web beacons (also known as pixel tags) are small invisible images embedded in web pages or emails that allow the Company to understand whether a specific page was visited or an email was opened.
    40→Users can control or disable cookies through their web browser settings. However, if cookies are disabled, some features of the Service (such as maintaining login status or saving preferences) may not function properly. The Company may also use third-party analytics services (for example, Google Analytics) that utilize cookies and similar technologies to collect usage data in an aggregate form. Any data collected via such analytics services will be used only to analyze and improve the Service and will be handled in accordance with the providers’ privacy policies and applicable laws.
    41→Article 10 (Disclosure, Correction, and Deletion of Personal Information) 
    42→1. Disclosure: If a User requests disclosure of their “retained personal data” (as defined by law) held by the Company, the Company will respond to the request without delay after confirming that the request is from the User themselves (or a legitimate representative of the User). However, the Company may refuse to disclose all or part of the information in the following cases: (a) if disclosure could potentially harm the life, body, property, or other rights or interests of the User or a third party; (b) if disclosure would significantly impede the proper operation of the Company’s business; (c) if disclosure would violate any other laws or regulations; or (d) if the data requested is not considered “retained personal data” subject to the disclosure obligation under the law. If the Company decides not to disclose the information in response to a User’s request, the Company will inform the User of the reason for the refusal in accordance with applicable laws.
    43→2. Information provided by the User: e.g. name, address, telephone number, email address, date of birth, and other profile or contact information that the User enters when registering for or using the Service. Information related to Service usage: e.g. usage history of the Service, access logs, device information, cookies and similar identifiers, and any data (including images, videos, or performance metrics) that the User uploads or inputs into the Service, including play videos (such as pitching and batting actions, among others).

Compliance and enforcement: To monitor and enforce compliance with the Terms of Use and this Policy, to investigate and prevent any improper or unauthorized activities, and to protect the rights, property, or safety of the Company, the Users, or others.
Billing and payment: To bill and process payments for Users utilizing paid services.
Other purposes related to or incidental to the above: To use the information for any purpose that is ancillary to the above purposes of use, to the extent permitted by law.
The Company will not use Personal Information beyond the scope necessary for achieving the above purposes without obtaining the User’s prior consent, except where permitted by law.

Article 4 (Provision of Personal Information to Third Parties)  The Company will not provide, exchange, sell, or otherwise transfer a User’s Personal Information to any third party without the User’s consent, except where permitted by law. However, the following circumstances may necessitate the provision of Personal Information to third parties:
When the Company entrusts the handling of Personal Information to a third party within the scope necessary to achieve the intended purposes of use.
When Personal Information is provided as part of a business transfer due to a merger or other reasons.
When permitted by applicable laws and regulations.
Article 5 (Outsourcing of Personal Information Handling)
When the Company outsources any part of the handling of Personal Information to external service providers (for example, cloud storage providers or data processors) in order to facilitate the operation of the Service, the Company will carefully select providers that have sufficient standards for protecting Personal Information. In such cases, the Company will enter into appropriate agreements with the providers and exercise necessary and appropriate supervision over them to ensure that the outsourced Personal Information is managed in a safe and proper manner.

Article 6 (Use of Anonymized Information and Statistical Data) To protect the Personal Information of Users, the Company may create and use anonymized information that has been processed so that specific individuals cannot be identified. Additionally, the Company may aggregate and analyze Personal Information of Users to create statistical data that cannot identify individuals, which may be used internally or provided or disclosed to third parties. These anonymized information and statistical data will be handled with due care to ensure that individuals cannot be identified, and the creation and provision of anonymized information will comply with procedures and standards prescribed by law.

Article 7 (Management and Protection of Personal Information)
The Company will handle Personal Information securely and implement appropriate organizational, physical, technical, and human security measures to protect Personal Information from unauthorized access, loss, destruction, falsification, leakage, or other risks. These measures include managing access controls to Personal Information, encrypting sensitive data, and providing training to employees on proper handling of Personal Information.
Article 8 (Storage Location and Transfer of Personal Information Overseas) The Personal Information of Users acquired by the Company is primarily stored on servers located in Japan. However, in the course of providing the Service, the Company may utilize cloud service servers located outside of Japan, including in the United States, and Personal Information may be stored or transferred to these overseas servers. In such cases, the Company will handle the information in accordance with applicable laws and take necessary measures, such as obtaining User consent and verifying the safety management measures of the recipient, when providing Personal Information to third parties overseas.
Article 9 (Use of Cookies and Similar Technologies)
The Service may use cookies, web beacons, and similar tracking technologies to enhance User experience and analyze User behavior. Cookies are small data files stored on a User’s device that allow the Service to remember the User’s preferences and activities. Web beacons (also known as pixel tags) are small invisible images embedded in web pages or emails that allow the Company to understand whether a specific page was visited or an email was opened.
Users can control or disable cookies through their web browser settings. However, if cookies are disabled, some features of the Service (such as maintaining login status or saving preferences) may not function properly. The Company may also use third-party analytics services (for example, Google Analytics) that utilize cookies and similar technologies to collect usage data in an aggregate form. Any data collected via such analytics services will be used only to analyze and improve the Service and will be handled in accordance with the providers’ privacy policies and applicable laws.
Article 10 (Disclosure, Correction, and Deletion of Personal Information) 
1. Disclosure: If a User requests disclosure of their “retained personal data” (as defined by law) held by the Company, the Company will respond to the request without delay after confirming that the request is from the User themselves (or a legitimate representative of the User). However, the Company may refuse to disclose all or part of the information in the following cases: (a) if disclosure could potentially harm the life, body, property, or other rights or interests of the User or a third party; (b) if disclosure would significantly impede the proper operation of the Company’s business; (c) if disclosure would violate any other laws or regulations; or (d) if the data requested is not considered “retained personal data” subject to the disclosure obligation under the law. If the Company decides not to disclose the information in response to a User’s request, the Company will inform the User of the reason for the refusal in accordance with applicable laws.
2. Correction/Additions/Deletion: If a User finds that the Personal Information held by the Company is incorrect or outdated, and requests correction, addition, or deletion of that information, the Company will investigate the request to the extent necessary for its purpose. Upon confirming the necessity based on the results of the investigation, the Company will correct, add, or delete the Personal Information without delay and inform the User of the changes. If the Company decides not to make the requested change, the Company will explain the reason to the User if required.
3. Suspension of Use, etc.: If a User requests the suspension of use or erasure of their Personal Information (e.g., for reasons such as the information being handled beyond the scope of the stated purpose of use or obtained by unlawful means), or requests the cessation of provision of their Personal Information to third parties, the Company will investigate the request. If the Company finds valid grounds under the law that require complying with the request, the Company will suspend use, erase the information, or cease provision to third parties as appropriate without delay, and notify the User of such action. If the Company decides not to honor the request (for example, if the Company is legally permitted to continue retaining the information), the Company will notify the User with an explanation if required by law.  For any account that is terminated by the Company or for which a request to close the account from a User is received by the Company, the Company shall remove all Personal Information related to that account within the later of (i) 30 days from such termination or receipt of such request; or (ii) the end of the Deletion Period (as defined in the Terms). The Company may retain User contact information in an irreversible hashed format for a certain period of time (up to one year) to prevent re-registration of Users who are suspended or terminated by the Company. 
4. Contact for Requests: Procedures for making any of the requests described in this Article (disclosure, correction, deletion, suspension, etc.) will be guided by the Company. For specifics on how to make such requests, please contact the Company at the inquiry contact point provided in Article 12. Please note that the Company may charge the fee permitted by law for processing a request for disclosure or notification of purpose of use. 

Article 11 (Personal Information of Minors) The Company will handle Personal Information obtained from minor Users in the same appropriate manner as it does for adult Users.  Children under the age of 13 are strictly prohibited from using the Service and only the parent or legal guardian of that child under 13 may use the Service.   For Users over the age of 13 but under the age of majority in their province, state or territory where they reside in to use the Service and provide Personal Information, a parent or legal guardian must provide consent and review this Policy and the Terms.  In accordance with the Terms, any parent or legal guardian that consents to the use of the Service by their minor child hereby agrees to bind the minor to the Terms and this Policy to fully indemnify and hold harmless the Company if the minor breaches any of the Terms or this Policy.   
The Service complies with the requirements of COPPA (Children’s Online Privacy Protection Act), and does not knowingly collect any information from anyone under 13 years of age (except that the Company may retain the minimum amount of information necessary to 'remember' a user under 13 years of age and block access), and will only knowingly collect Personal Information about Users over 13 but under the age of majority with prior parental or legal guardian consent in accordance with this Policy and the Terms. The Service is directed to people who are at least 18 years old or older, and any use of the Service by anyone under 13 is prohibited.  Any User that uploads, submits, or otherwise provides User Content that contains information about anyone under the age of 18 must receive affirmative consent from such person under 18's parent or legal guardian for any and all uses of such User Content by that User, including, but not limited to, sharing any video, audio, or Personal Information in any way or medium.  The Company may request any such consents at any time, and if User cannot produce such consents, the Company reserves all rights to suspend or terminate any portion of or all of a User's account, data, Content, Modified Content or any other materials associated with the User.  Users are solely responsible for compliance with all applicable laws related to uploading, submitting or otherwise providing User Content, especially any Personal Information, that contains any persons under 18.  Registered Users must be over the age of 13 and for Users over 13 and under the age of majority, consent of a parent or legal guardian must be provided after the parent or guardian.
Article 12 (Contact for Inquiries) 
For any questions, concerns, or requests regarding this Privacy Policy or the Company’s handling of Personal Information (including requests for disclosure, correction, suspension of use, or deletion as described in Article 10), please contact the Company at the following:
Personal Information Inquiry Desk – Knowhere Inc. (E-mail: info@knowhere.co.jp ) (Postal Mail: info@knowhere.co.jp)
Article 13 (Changes to this Policy)
The Company may amend this Policy from time to time as necessary to reflect changes in law or changes in the Service or Company practices. When the Company makes significant changes to this Policy, the Company will notify Users of the updated Policy and its effective date by an appropriate method (such as posting a notice on the Service website or within the app, or sending an email notification to Users). If required by applicable law, the Company will also seek the User’s consent for material changes that involve new uses or disclosures of Personal Information.
Unless otherwise specified by the Company, the revised Policy will take effect as of the effective date indicated at the time of publication. Users are encouraged to review the latest version of this Policy periodically. Continued use of the Service after the effective date of any updated Policy will be deemed as acceptance of the changes, except where explicit consent is required.

Article 14 (Effective Date and Revision Date)
Effective Date: September 10, 2025
Revision Date: September 10, 2025

Article 15 (US State Law Provisions)
The Company will not discriminate against a User that chooses to exercise any rights related to a User's privacy or Personal Information. The Company will not deny goods or services, charge different prices or rates, provide a different level of service or quality of goods, or suggest that a User might receive a different price or level of quality for goods as a result of User exercising any rights herein.

Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Iowa, Montana, Nebraska, New Hampshire, Oregon, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, and Virginia
These additional rights and disclosures may apply to residents of Colorado, Connecticut, Delaware, Iowa, Montana, Nebraska, New Hampshire, Oregon, Tennessee, Texas, Utah, and Virginia.
Data Subject Requests
If a User is a resident of these states, these state's law may provide some or all of the following rights:
To confirm whether or not the Company is processing User's personal data;
To access User's personal data;
To correct inaccuracies in User's personal data;
To delete User's personal data;
To obtain a copy of User's personal data that User previously provided to the Company in a portable and readily usable format; or
To obtain a list of third parties to which the Company disclosed User's personal data.
To exercise any of these rights, a User must submit a request as provided for in Article 12. Please note these rights are subject to exceptions. If a User has an account with the Company, the Company may require User to use the account to submit the request. In the request, please specify which right User is seeking to exercise and the scope of the request. The Company will confirm receipt of the request within 10 days and respond to the request within 45 days. To verify User identity, the Company may require a User to confirm receipt of an email sent to an email address that matches Company records, provide the Company with details relating to User's history with the Company, or provide additional information. If the Company is unable to verify a User's identity, the Company may deny User's request.
Sales and Targeted Advertising
The Company does not disclose or otherwise sell personal data (e.g., identifiers and service and platform usage) or Personal Information to any third party for the purposes of selling such personal data or Personal Information or for providing targeted or behavioral advertising. 
Profiling
The Company does not process personal data or Personal Information for purposes of profiling in furtherance of decisions that produce legal or similarly significant effects concerning consumers.
Designated Methods and Authorized Agents
All rights requests must be exercised through the designated methods listed above. A User may make a rights request using an authorized agent. The Company requires written and signed proof of the agent’s permission to do so and the Company may verify User identity directly with the User.
Appeal
A User may have the right to appeal the Company's denial of User's request. To appeal, please contact the Company at the email address provided in Article 12 and specify what the User would like to appeal. The Company will review and respond to the appeal in accordance with applicable law. If the Company denies the appeal, the User may submit a complaint as follows:
For Colorado residents, visit https://coag.gov/file-complaint/
For Connecticut residents, visit https://www.dir.ct.gov/ag/complaint/
For Delaware residents, visit https://attorneygeneral.delaware.gov/fraud/cmu/complaint/
For Iowa residents, visit https://www.iowaattorneygeneral.gov/for-consumers/file-a-consumer-complaint
For Montana residents, visit https://dojmt.gov/office-of-consumer-protection/consumer-complaints/
For Nebraska residents, visit https://www.nebraska.gov/apps-ago-complaints/
For New Hampshire residents, visit https://www.doj.nh.gov/citizens/consumer-protection-antitrust-bureau/consumer-complaints
For Oregon residents, visit https://www.doj.state.or.us/oregon-department-of-justice/contact-us/
For Tennessee residents, visit https://www.tn.gov/attorneygeneral/working-for-tennessee/consumer/file-a-complaint
For Texas residents, visit https://www.texasattorneygeneral.gov/consumer-protection/file-consumer-complaint
For Utah residents, visit https://attorneygeneral.utah.gov/contact/complaint-form/
For Virginia residents, visit https://www.oag.state.va.us/consumercomplaintform

• Information provided by the User: e.g. name, address, telephone number, email address, date of birth, and other profile or contact information that the User enters when registering for or using the Service.
• Information related to Service usage: e.g. usage history of the Service, access logs, device information, cookies and similar identifiers, and any data (including images, videos, or performance metrics) that the User uploads or inputs into the Service, including play videos (such as pitching and batting actions, among others).
• Location information: Device location data obtained when the User permits the use of location services within the Service.
• Payment information: Information related to credit card details and other payment methods collected when purchasing paid content or making transactions within the Service, including information obtained through external services such as Apple or Stripe necessary for payment processing.

+        content: 'The Company will acquire Personal Information by appropriate and lawful means. The Company uses the information collected for a variety of purposes. The Company's legal bases for processing a User's Personal Information are: 1) the Company's legitimate interest in running and maintaining the Service and the Company's business; 2) performance and fulfillment of contracts; 3) a User's consent; and 4) compliance with legal obligations. In many instances, more than one of these legal bases apply to the processing of a User's Personal Information.  The Company may collect the following Personal Information from Users when they use the Service or when such information is provided by Users:

Information provided by the User: e.g. name, address, telephone number, email address, date of birth, and other profile or contact information that the User enters when registering for or using the Service.  Users may also submit audio, video, and other data related to a User's athletic profile, which will be treated Personal Information for the purposes of this Policy and the Terms.

Location information: Device location data obtained when the User permits the use of location services within the Service.

Payment information: Information related to credit card details and other payment methods collected when purchasing paid content or making transactions within the Service, including information obtained through external services such as Apple or Stripe necessary for payment processing.

Other information obtained in connection with the User's use of the Service: Any additional Personal Information that the Company needs to collect for operating or improving the Service, which will be obtained by appropriate means and with prior notice to the User when required.'
title: 'Article 3 (Purpose of Use of Personal Information)',

-        content: 'The Company will use the collected personal information for the following purposes:

1. Provision and operation of the Service: To allow Users to fully utilize the Service's features, maintain and administer User accounts, and provide any services or functionality available through the Service, including features for collaboration with scouts to ensure smooth use of the Service.
2. Identity verification and security: To verify User identity when logging into the Service or when otherwise necessary, and to ensure the security of the Service by preventing unauthorized access and fraud.
3. Customer support and communication: To respond to inquiries, support requests, or complaints from Users, and to provide guidance or notices regarding the use of the Service.
4. Notifications and updates: To send Users important notices regarding the Service (such as changes in terms, maintenance notifications, or security alerts) and to inform Users about new features, updates, or related services offered by the Company.
5. Marketing and information provision (with consent as required): To send newsletters, surveys, promotional materials or other information regarding the Service or the Company's related products and services that may be of interest to the User. (The Company will obtain the User's consent in accordance with applicable laws before sending marketing communications when such consent is required.)
6. Improvement and analysis: To analyze usage of the Service (including creating statistical data that has been anonymized) in order to maintain, improve, or enhance the quality of the Service and to develop new services.
7. Advertisements and promotional contents: To display and distribute advertisements and promotional contents tailored to Users on the Service or through partnered third-party media.
8. Compliance and enforcement: To monitor and enforce compliance with the Terms of Use and this Policy, to investigate and prevent any improper or unauthorized activities, and to protect the rights, property, or safety of the Company, the Users, or others.
9. Billing and payment: To bill and process payments for Users utilizing paid services.
10. Other purposes related to or incidental to the above: To use the information for any purpose that is ancillary to the above purposes of use, to the extent permitted by law.

The Company will not use personal information beyond the scope necessary for achieving the above purposes without obtaining the User's prior consent, except where permitted by law.'

-        content: '1. Disclosure: If a User requests disclosure of their "retained personal data" (as defined by law) held by the Company, the Company will respond to the request without delay after confirming that the request is from the User themselves (or a legitimate representative of the User). However, the Company may refuse to disclose all or part of the information in the following cases: (a) if disclosure could potentially harm the life, body, property, or other rights or interests of the User or a third party; (b) if disclosure would significantly impede the proper operation of the Company's business; (c) if disclosure would violate any other laws or regulations; or (d) if the data requested is not considered "retained personal data" subject to the disclosure obligation under the law. If the Company decides not to disclose the information in response to a User's request, the Company will inform the User of the reason for the refusal in accordance with applicable laws.

2. Correction/Additions/Deletion: If a User finds that the personal information held by the Company is incorrect or outdated, and requests correction, addition, or deletion of that information, the Company will investigate the request to the extent necessary for its purpose. Upon confirming the necessity based on the results of the investigation, the Company will correct, add, or delete the personal information without delay and inform the User of the changes. If the Company decides not to make the requested change, the Company will explain the reason to the User if required.

3. Suspension of Use, etc.: If a User requests the suspension of use or erasure of their personal information (e.g., for reasons such as the information being handled beyond the scope of the stated purpose of use or obtained by unlawful means), or requests the cessation of provision of their personal information to third parties, the Company will investigate the request. If the Company finds valid grounds under the law that require complying with the request, the Company will suspend use, erase the information, or cease provision to third parties as appropriate without delay, and notify the User of such action. If the Company decides not to honor the request (for example, if the Company is legally permitted to continue retaining the information), the Company will notify the User with an explanation if required by law.

4. Contact for Requests: Procedures for making any of the requests described in this Article (disclosure, correction, deletion, suspension, etc.) will be guided by the Company. For specifics on how to make such requests, please contact the Company at the inquiry contact point provided in Article 12. Please note that the Company may charge the fee permitted by law for processing a request for disclosure or notification of purpose of use.'

content.text:
privacy-policy.tsx の英語版を policy.md の内容に更新しました。

主な変更点：

- **冒頭**：米国法や他の適用法への準拠を明記
- **Article 1**：Personal Information の定義に video data、audio data、athletic characteristics を追加
- **Article 2**：法的根拠（legitimate interest、契約履行、同意、法的義務）を追加、ユーザーが提出する audio/video データの説明を追加
- **Article 3**：personalization、customer service、contest/survey の管理などの目的を追加
- **Article 4**：provide だけでなく exchange、sell、otherwise transfer も禁止と明記
- **Article 10-3**：アカウント削除時の 30 日以内の Personal Information 削除、ハッシュ化による再登録防止措置を追加
- **Article 11**：COPPA への準拠、13 歳未満の利用禁止、18 歳未満の User Content に関する保護者同意の詳細規定を追加
- **Article 12**：メールアドレスを info@knowhere.co.jp に変更、Postal Mail 情報を追加
- **Article 14**：有効日を September 10, 2025 に変更、日本語版優先の記述を削除
- **Article 15**：米国各州法の規定を全面追加（Colorado、Connecticut、Delaware など）、カナダ・中南米・韓国・台湾の個人情報保護に関する規定を追加

URL をクリック可能なリンクに変換しました。

変更内容：

1. **Link コンポーネントのインポート追加**：MUI から `Link` をインポート
2. **`renderContentWithLinks` 関数の追加**：テキスト内の URL を検出し、`Link` コンポーネントに変換
3. **Typography 内でリンク化関数を適用**：`{section.content}` を `{renderContentWithLinks(section.content)}` に変更

これにより、Article 15 の Appeal セクション内の各州の URL がクリック可能なリンクとして表示されます。
